# @vllnt/convex-suppression

> Do-not-contact suppression list + opt-in proof (GDPR/CAN-SPAM), hash-keyed and channel-aware, as a Convex component

`@vllnt/convex-suppression` is a publishable Convex component. It targets `convex@^1.41.0`.
It stores two sandboxed tables: `suppressions` (a `(contactHash, channel)` anti-membership tombstone with a `reason` of `unsubscribe`/`bounce`/`complaint`/`manual`/`global` and a `createdAt` server timestamp) and `optInProofs` (a `(contactHash, listKey)` record of a confirmed opt-in with a `source`, opaque host `proof`, and a `confirmedAt` server timestamp). The component is keyed by a host-supplied `contactHash` (`hash(normalize(email|phone))`) and never stores raw PII, so a "do not contact" tombstone survives erasure of the subject — the GDPR crux. A sender calls `isEligible(contactHash, { channel?, listKey?, requireOptIn? })` before every send (`¬suppressed [∧ confirmed]`); an unsubscribe/bounce/complaint calls `suppress(contactHash, reason, { channel? })`; a confirmed double opt-in calls `recordOptIn(contactHash, { listKey?, source, proof? })`; rare re-subscribes call `unsuppress`. A suppression with no channel is a global all-channel tombstone (stored under a `"*"` sentinel) that matches every channel; a channel entry matches only itself. Writes are idempotent on their key tuple (a replayed webhook updates in place, never duplicates). Suppression is explicitly the residue that is NOT a membership — a subscriber list is a flat membership edge or host data, modeled elsewhere. Time is read from the server clock inside every handler — no caller-supplied clock. The host owns the raw contact, the hashing + salt policy, auth, and the opt-in `proof` type (the client is generic over it, with an optional `proofValidator` parser narrowing the opaque stored value at the boundary — never a `v.any()` dump); the component owns only the hash-keyed do-not-contact gate. Mount-safe: it runs correctly under multiple named `app.use` mounts (e.g. a separate marketing vs. transactional list).
